name: golang-github-cloudflare-backoff-dev
title: golang-github-cloudflare-backoff-dev
summary: Go implementation of "Exponential Backoff And Jitter" (library)
description:
Go implementation of "Exponential Backoff And Jitter" (library)
This package implements the backoff strategy described in the AWS
Architecture Blog article "Exponential Backoff And Jitter"
(http://www.awsarchitectureblog.com/2015/03/backoff.html). Essentially,
the backoff has an interval time.Duration; the *nth* call to backoff
will return an a time.Duration that is *2 n *interval*. If jitter is
enabled (which is the default behaviour), the duration is a random value
between 0 and *2 n * interval*. The backoff is configured with a maximum
duration that will not be exceeded; e.g., by default, the longest
duration returned is backoff.DefaultMaxDuration.
use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use golang-github-cloudflare-backoff-dev because Go implementation of "Exponential Backoff And Jitter" (library).
When the engagement is in the install phase, use golang-github-cloudflare-backoff-dev as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair golang-github-cloudflare-backoff-dev with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `golang-github-cloudflare-backoff-dev --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
